videos.title: A Better Way to Handle Power BI Report Design! (Organizational Themes)
videos.description:
Organizational Themes let Power BI tenant admins centrally manage and publish branded theme JSON files so report creators can apply approved themes directly in Power BI Desktop and the Power BI service! No more emailing files or guessing which version is latest. 

In this video Marthe shows you:
✅ Uploading a theme in the Fabric admin portal
✅ Enabling it for the Theme gallery
✅ Applying it in Desktop + Service

Organizational themes (Preview): https://learn.microsoft.com/en-us/power-bi/create-r

videos.title: April release of Power BI is so so so good if you want a free design update 😍🔥 #PowerBI
AI Agents: Three Main Categories
videos.description:
Summary

The conversation explores the frontier transformation pattern and its relevance to agent usage categories and patterns. It delves into the ways people use agents and identifies three main categories, as well as five patterns around these categories.

Takeaways

* Agent usage categories
* Patterns of agent usage

Chapters

* 00:00 Agent Usage Categories

videos.title: Power Up: Learn AI by Doing
videos.description:
Summary

The conversation introduces Power Up as a tool to help people try AI and build confidence through hands-on experience. It emphasizes the accessibility of AI for everyone and the empowerment that comes from learning by doing.

Takeaways

* AI for everyone
* Confidence through doing

Chapters

* 00:00 Introduction to Power Up and AI

videos.title: Who Cares About Consumer AI
videos.description:
Video analyzes the clash between consumer and enterprise AI, highlighting Coinbase's AI framing of layoffs and Meta's consumer-agent project Hatch. Product and model updates covered include OpenAI's GPT-5.5 Instant and a shift toward coding and enterprise use cases. Market implications include Anthropic's reported $200 billion Google Cloud compute commitment, Palantir's revenue surge, and debates over ads, agentic commerce, token scarcity, and compute as a tradable commodity.

The AI Daily Brief

videos.title: Is AI Doom Going Out of Style?
videos.description:
Faint but converging signals suggest the AI doom narrative may finally be cracking — and they're showing up in the chattering class and the markets at the same time. This episode walks through the evidence: Ezra Klein's New York Times pushback on the AI job apocalypse, Alex Imas's scarcity framework, Atlassian's blowout earnings, and Sam Altman's rhetorical pivot from replacement to augmentation.

The AI Daily Brief helps you understand the most important news and discussions in AI.
